title: How to get a police certificate – Uruguay - Canada.ca
content:
## How to get a police certificate – Uruguay
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

## Name of the document you need
Certificado de Antecedentes Judiciales

## Police certificate and IRCC requirements
You must provide a police certificate when you apply for:
- some temporary residence programs
Check your application package or instructions for details.

### If you live in Uruguay
Apply to the National Directorate of Scientific Police ( Dirección Nacional de Policía Científica ).
For more information, visit the Tramites website .

### If you live outside of Uruguay
You’ll need someone in Uruguay to apply for you. You must go to your nearest Uruguay embassy or consulate to
- pay the application fee
- have your fingerprints taken
- name someone to represent you in Uruguay
- send your application and fingerprints to your representative so they can apply at the National Directorate of Scientific Police ( Dirección Nacional de Policía Científica ) for you

### What you need to apply
- Cedula de Identidad document

## Special considerations
Only certificates issued by the National Directorate of Scientific Police are accepted. Certificates issued by any other federal/provincial government offices are not accepted.
Ministerio del Interior
Phone: 00598 - 21523300 to 3303
Email: certificacionesdnpt@adinet.com.uy
